question_text: A cyclist travels 15 km in 30 minutes on a flat, straight road. Calculate the average speed of the cyclist in m/s and identify whether this speed is constant or changing throughout the journey.
correct_answer: Average speed = distance ÷ time = 15 km ÷ 0.5 hours = 30 km/h = 30 × (1000/3600) = 8.33 m/s. We cannot determine if speed is constant or changing from average speed alone; the cyclist may have accelerated, decelerated, or maintained constant speed.
explanation: Average speed is total distance divided by total time. However, average speed tells us nothing about what happened during the journey—the cyclist's instantaneous speed could have varied throughout.
